chunk_text: Full-time employees receive 20 paid annual leave days per calendar year. Employees may carry over at most 5 unused annual leave days, and carried days expire on March 31 of the following year.
Regular employees receive 10 paid sick days per calendar year. An employee may take up to 5 paid working days of bereavement leave for the death of an immediate family member.
A potential conflict of interest must be disclosed to Ethics within 5 business days of discovery. Gifts from a single vendor may not exceed an aggregate value of USD 75 in a calendar year.
Core collaboration hours are 10:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m. local time, Monday through Thursday. Weekly time records must be submitted by 6:00 p.m. local time each Friday.
Enterprise passwords must contain at least 14 characters. Multi-factor authentication is mandatory for remote access and every privileged account.
Restricted data must use AES-256 encryption at rest and TLS 1.2 or later in transit. The Communications team must approve information before it is classified as Public.

A suspected information-security incident must be reported to the Security Operations Center within 30 minutes of detection. A suspected phishing message must be forwarded as an attachment to security@example.test without clicking links or replying.

System owners must review privileged access rights every quarter. Access must be disabled within 4 hours for an involuntary separation and by the end of the last working day for a voluntary separation.
Airfare should be booked at least 21 calendar days before departure when practicable. Economy class is required for flight segments shorter than 6 hours.
The nightly lodging cap is USD 220 in standard locations and USD 320 in designated high-cost cities, before tax. Business-travel meals are reimbursable up to USD 75 per day, including tips.

Approved use of a personal vehicle is reimbursed at USD 0.67 per business mile. Employees may expense a standard rideshare tier; premium and luxury tiers are not reimbursable unless required as an accommodation.

An expense report must be submitted within 10 business days after the traveler returns. Travel expected to exceed USD 5,000 requires written preapproval from the employee's director and Finance Operations.

Tier 1 critical services have a 4-hour recovery time objective and a 1-hour recovery point objective. Tier 2 important services have a 24-hour recovery time objective and an 8-hour recovery point objective.
The designated Incident Commander has authority to activate the business continuity plan. Mass notification must begin within 15 minutes after the business continuity plan is activated.
Each business unit must complete a tabletop continuity exercise every quarter. Every Tier 1 service must complete a production failover test at least once each calendar year.

Only the Crisis Communications Lead or a formally delegated spokesperson may make external statements about a continuity incident. For a Severity 1 continuity incident, internal status updates must be issued at least every 60 minutes.
A purchase below USD 5,000 requires one documented supplier quote. A purchase from USD 5,000 through USD 25,000 requires at least three competitive quotes.

A purchase above USD 25,000 requires a formal request for proposals managed by Strategic Sourcing. A purchase above USD 100,000 requires approval from both the Chief Financial Officer and the Procurement Director.

A vendor security risk assessment must be approved before a supplier receives Restricted data or production-system access. Strategic Sourcing screens every active vendor against sanctions lists at least annually.

Only an officer listed in the corporate authority matrix may sign a supplier contract; a project role alone does not grant signing authority. Procurement records must be retained for 7 years after contract expiration or termination.

A new employee becomes eligible for a regular remote schedule after 90 calendar days of employment and a satisfactory performance standing. An approved hybrid employee may normally work remotely up to 3 days per workweek.

Remote work must remain within the employee's employing country unless Legal, Tax, Information Security, and People Operations give written approval. A remote-location change lasting more than 30 days requires notice to People Operations at least 20 business days in advance.

Employees must use a company-managed laptop for remote work; personal computers may not access company systems. An approved remote worker may receive a home-internet stipend of up to USD 60 per month.

Remote workers must use a private workspace or a headset that prevents confidential conversations from being overheard. Remote employees must be reachable during the core hours of 10:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m. in their local time zone, Monday through Thursday.
Health coverage begins on the first day of the month after the employee's start date. A new employee has 30 calendar days from the start date to complete benefits enrollment.
The company contributes 4% of eligible pay when an employee contributes at least 5% of eligible pay to the retirement plan. Company retirement-plan matching contributions are immediately 100% vested.

Eligible employees may receive up to USD 600 per calendar year for approved wellness expenses. After 6 months of service, an employee may receive up to USD 5,000 per calendar year in approved tuition assistance.

An eligible parent receives 12 weeks of paid parental leave for a birth, adoption, or foster placement. The short-term disability plan replaces 60% of base pay for up to 26 weeks, subject to the plan maximum.

A formal project charter is required when estimated effort exceeds 100 person-hours or total spend exceeds USD 25,000. The accountable executive sponsor must approve the project charter before detailed planning begins.

The project manager and core team must review the active risk register at least every two weeks. A change that increases approved cost or schedule by more than 10% requires steering committee approval before implementation.
The project manager must publish the weekly status report by 12:00 noon local time each Friday. A Red project status must be escalated to the sponsor and PMO within 1 business day.

The business owner must sign the final acceptance record before the project is administratively closed. The project team must complete a lessons-learned review within 10 business days after final acceptance.
The primary on-call engineer must acknowledge a Severity 1 page within 5 minutes. The primary on-call engineer must acknowledge a Severity 2 page within 15 minutes.

An Incident Commander must be assigned if a Severity 1 incident remains unresolved 10 minutes after acknowledgement. The first executive update for a Severity 1 incident is due within 30 minutes of incident declaration.

Customer-facing status updates must be posted at least every 30 minutes during an active Severity 1 incident. A Severity 1 postmortem must be completed within 5 business days after service restoration.
The daily on-call handoff occurs at 9:00 a.m. in the receiving engineer's local time zone. An engineer may not serve as primary on-call for more than 7 consecutive calendar days.
During a fire alarm, occupants must use the nearest safe stairwell and must not use elevators. The primary headquarters evacuation muster point is North Parking Lot Zone B.
A lost access badge must be reported to Workplace Security within 15 minutes of discovery. A visitor in a secured office area must display a temporary badge and remain escorted by an employee host.

For a life-threatening workplace injury, call local emergency services first and then notify Workplace Security. A non-urgent workplace injury must be reported in the safety portal before the end of the injured employee's work shift.
Workplace Services and People Operations normally announce a headquarters weather closure by 6:00 a.m. local time. Workplace Services reviews an ergonomic assessment request within 5 business days.
A former employee's personnel file is retained for 7 years after termination of employment. Records for an unsuccessful job applicant are retained for 2 years after the hiring decision.
Supplier invoices and payment support are retained for 7 fiscal years after the fiscal year of payment. Filed corporate tax returns and supporting workpapers are retained for 10 years after filing.
A customer support ticket is retained for 3 years after the ticket is closed. A recorded customer support call is retained for 18 months after the recording date.

The automated retention purge runs once each quarter for records whose retention period has expired. A legal hold suspends scheduled deletion until the Legal Department issues a written release of the hold.
A Priority 1 customer case has a 15-minute initial response target, available 24 hours a day and 7 days a week. A Priority 2 customer case has an initial response target of 2 business hours.

A Priority 1 case must be escalated to the support manager if it remains unresolved 30 minutes after opening. A support case involving suspected privacy or security impact must be escalated immediately to the Trust Response team.

The assigned case owner must update the customer at least every 60 minutes while a Priority 1 case remains active. A written resolution summary must be sent to the customer within 2 business days after case resolution.

A support agent may approve a goodwill credit up to USD 100; a support manager may approve up to USD 500. A substantive customer interaction must be documented in the case record by the end of the same business day.
